content.cn_text: 你将听到两名学生 Amy 和 Mike 讨论毛利雕刻。
content.en_text: You will hear two students named Amy and Mike discussing Maori Carvings.

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间浏览第21至第25题。
content.en_text: First, you have some time to look at questions 21 to 25.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听，并回答第21至第25题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 25.

content.cn_text: Amy：嗨，Mike。我们课堂上学习的这些新西兰雕刻是不是很迷人？
content.en_text: Amy: Hi, Mike. Aren't these New Zealand carvings we've been studying in class fascinating?

content.cn_text: Mike：哦，嗨 Amy。你说毛利绿石 tiki 啊。对我来说，它们有点太自然有机了。
content.en_text: Mike: Oh, hi, Amy. Oh, the Maori greenstone tikis. They're all a bit organic for my taste.

content.cn_text: 你知道，就是全是曲线和扭转形状。
content.en_text: You know, all curves and twisting shapes.

content.cn_text: 我喜欢现代的东西，比如几何形状，但我能看出制作它们所需的技艺。
content.en_text: I like modern stuff, geometric shapes, but I can see the skill involved in making them.

content.cn_text: Amy：但让它们有趣的是 tiki 背后的故事。
content.en_text: Amy: But the stories behind the tikis are what make them interesting.

content.cn_text: 而且，我想创造几何图案会很困难。
content.en_text: Besides, I imagine it would be difficult to create geometric patterns.

content.cn_text: 每个 tiki 的形状似乎都顺着石头的自然轮廓。
content.en_text: The shape of each tiki seems to follow the natural contours of the stone.

content.cn_text: 它非常坚硬，而且塑形需要专业技术和时间。
content.en_text: It's extremely hard, and it requires expertise and time to shape.

content.cn_text: Mike：没错。我想这就是为什么现代绿石雕刻这么贵。
content.en_text: Mike: That's true. I suppose that's why modern greenstone carvings are so expensive.

content.cn_text: 但真正古老的 tiki 并不多见。
content.en_text: But there aren't that many genuine old ones around.

content.cn_text: 考古学家很少发现这样的珍宝，考虑到它们是毛利文化的重要组成部分，这有点奇怪。
content.en_text: Archaeologists rarely discover such treasures, which is odd, given they are such a huge part of Maori culture.

content.cn_text: 我在想是不是考古发掘地点被洗劫，tiki 被拿去卖了？
content.en_text: I wonder if archaeological dig sites were raided, and the tikis were taken to sell?

content.cn_text: Amy：我认为这是因为拥有者很珍视它们，所以把它们保存了下来。
content.en_text: Amy: I believe it's because the owners valued them, and so preserved them.

content.cn_text: 其中许多会代代相传，像传家宝一样至今仍由家族持有。
content.en_text: Many of them would have been passed down through the generations and remain in the possession of the families today, like heirlooms.

content.cn_text: 你知道它们是用来做什么的吗？
content.en_text: Do you know what they were used for?

content.cn_text: Mike: 我以为它们只是某种艺术形式，或者一种装饰。
content.en_text: Mike: I thought they were just an art form, or a means of decoration.

content.cn_text: 但 Matiu 教授说，毛利人认为 tiki 是神圣的，可以作为通向祖先的通道。
content.en_text: But Professor Matiu says that the Maori believed tikis were sacred, and could be used as a pathway to their ancestors.

content.cn_text: 他们相信逝者可以通过这些护身符与他们交流。
content.en_text: They believed the dead could speak to them through these talismans.

content.cn_text: Amy：所以它们不是代表部落之类的吗？
content.en_text: Amy: So they don't represent tribes or anything?

content.cn_text: 有意思。有多少不同种类？
content.en_text: Interesting. How many different kinds are there?

content.cn_text: 肯定有数百种变体。
content.en_text: There must be hundreds of variations.

content.cn_text: Mike: 除了 tiki 之外，还有其他绿石雕刻，虽然我记不住所有名字。
content.en_text: Mike: There are other greenstone carvings beside tikis, although I can't remember all their names.

content.cn_text: 不过传统 tiki 本身可以分为两个不同类别。
content.en_text: Traditional tikis themselves can be divided into two distinct groups though.

content.cn_text: Amy：它们是按材料分类的吗？
content.en_text: Amy: Can they be divided by the materials?

content.cn_text: 我见过一些有金属特征的，或者是和尺寸、产地有关？
content.en_text: I've seen some with metal features, or is it to do with size, or where they were made?

content.cn_text: Mike: 实际上，主要区别在于它们的脚、头和手的摆放方式。
content.en_text: Mike: Actually, it is the ways that their feet, heads and hands are placed that make the major difference.

content.cn_text: Amy：容易分辨古代石雕和现代复制品的区别吗？
content.en_text: Amy: Is it easy to discern the difference between the ancient stones and more modern reproductions?

content.cn_text: 风格差异很大吗？
content.en_text: Are the styles very different?

content.cn_text: Mike: 现代复制品通常使用不同类型的石头，虽然设计可能相同。
content.en_text: Mike: Modern reproductions usually use a different type of stone, although they may be of the same design.

content.cn_text: 它们尺寸也可能更小。
content.en_text: They may also be of a smaller size.

content.cn_text: 但如果你是在买真正的雕刻还是大批量生产的纪念品，其实很容易分辨。
content.en_text: But it's actually quite easy to tell if you are buying a genuine carving or a mass-produced souvenir.

content.cn_text: Amy：怎么分辨？
content.en_text: Amy: How?

content.cn_text: Mike: 如今它们是机器生产的，甚至使用激光，所以它们没有瑕疵。
content.en_text: Mike: Nowadays, they are produced by machine, using lasers even, so they have no blemishes.

content.cn_text: 真正的雕刻并不总是完美，因为毛利人使用简单的手工工具。
content.en_text: In genuine carvings, they aren't always perfect, because the Maori used simple hand tools.

content.cn_text: 例如，穿绳子的孔并不是完全圆形。
content.en_text: For example, the hole that the cord is fed through isn't perfectly round.

content.cn_text: Amy：这说得通。
content.en_text: Amy: That makes sense.

content.cn_text: 在听剩余讨论之前，你有一些时间浏览第26至第30题。
content.en_text: Before you hear the rest of the discussion, you have some time to look at questions 26-30.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听，并回答第26至第30题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 26-30.

content.cn_text: Amy：事实上，他们用这么简单的工具和材料制作出如此精细的 tiki，真令人惊叹。
content.en_text: Amy: Actually, it's amazing that they produce such detailed tikis with such simple tools and materials.

content.cn_text: 给我讲讲过程吧。
content.en_text: Talk me through the process.

content.cn_text: 我知道基本形状是从新西兰绿石上雕出来的。
content.en_text: I know the basic shape is carved from New Zealand greenstone.

content.cn_text: Mike: 是的，但首先他们必须得到大小合适的坯料。
content.en_text: Mike: Yes, but first they had to get a blank of the right size.

content.cn_text: Amy：你是说一块大小和基本形状合适的石头？
content.en_text: Amy: You mean a piece of stone of the right size and basic shape?

content.cn_text: Mike: 没错。
content.en_text: Mike: That's right.

content.cn_text: 他们用坚硬的石制工具，一种粗糙的东西，在绿石上磨出一道凹槽。
content.en_text: They used a hard stone tool, something rough, to wear a groove into a piece of greenstone.

content.cn_text: 用它划刻后，就可以把石头折成两半。
content.en_text: After they'd scored it with that, they could just snap it in two.

content.cn_text: 我猜他们也许可以用某种锤子。
content.en_text: I guess they could have used some kind of hammer though.

content.cn_text: 他们重复这个过程，直到得到一块适合雕刻目标的石头。
content.en_text: They repeated the process until they had a piece of stone suitable for what they wanted to carve.

content.cn_text: 通常是用于 tiki 的扁平石块。
content.en_text: Usually flat pieces for tikis.

content.cn_text: Amy：明白，因为它们相当小而且很薄，然后他们就开始雕刻。
content.en_text: Amy: Right, because they're fairly small and quite thin, then they started carving.

content.cn_text: Mike: 不是马上。
content.en_text: Mike: Not right away.

content.cn_text: 在开始雕刻前，他们需要一个绝对光滑平整的表面。
content.en_text: Before they could start carving, they needed an absolutely smooth flat surface.

content.cn_text: 如今，如果我们想做类似事情，会把石头夹在虎钳里，然后用砂光机。
content.en_text: Nowadays, when we want to do something like that, we'd clamp the stone in a vise and use a sander.

content.cn_text: 但他们使用一大块砂岩，把绿石在上面反复摩擦，为雕刻做准备。
content.en_text: But they used a heavy block of sandstone and rubbed the piece of greenstone on it over and over to prepare it for carving.

content.cn_text: Amy：这一定非常耗时。
content.en_text: Amy: It must have been very time consuming.

content.cn_text: 那他们怎么切出手脚之类的小细节？
content.en_text: So how did they cut out the little details like hands and feet?

content.cn_text: Mike: 他们有雕刻工具。
content.en_text: Mike: They had carving tools.

content.cn_text: 他们使用粘在并绑在木柄上的小石片。
content.en_text: They used small chips of stone glued and tied onto a wooden handle.

content.cn_text: 然后他们还使用一块磨尖成尖端的骨头。
content.en_text: Then they also used a piece of sharpened bone made into a point.

content.cn_text: Amy：他们也是用这个来打孔吗？
content.en_text: Amy: Is that what they used to make the holes?

content.cn_text: Mike: 实际上，他们有一种专门打孔的工具。
content.en_text: Mike: Actually, they had a special tool to make holes.

content.cn_text: 那是一根木棍，绑在两块重卵石之间，末端有尖头。
content.en_text: It was a stick tied between two heavy pebbles with a point at the end.

content.cn_text: 他们会把绳子绕在上面，来回拉动，在 tiki 上磨出一个孔。
content.en_text: They would wrap string around it, pull it back and forth to wear a hole into the tiki.

content.cn_text: 他们也用同样技术制作圆形，但不会让孔完全穿透。
content.en_text: They used the same technique to make round shapes too, but didn't make the hole go all the way through.

content.cn_text: Amy：我见过一些 tiki 有彩色眼睛或装饰。它们是画上去的吗？
content.en_text: Amy: And I've seen some tikis with coloured eyes or decorations. Are they painted?

content.cn_text: Mike: 大多数是镶嵌不同颜色的石头、贝壳里的珍珠母，或者有时是染色骨片。
content.en_text: Mike: Mostly they are inlaid with different coloured stone or mother of pearl from shells or sometimes dyed pieces of bone.

content.cn_text: 它们用 tarata gum 固定在原处，这是一种黏性的植物树脂。
content.en_text: They were fixed in place using tarata gum, a sticky plant resin.

content.cn_text: 他们也用植物制作天然绳子。
content.en_text: They used plants to make the natural strings too.

content.cn_text: Amy：没错，毛利人非常善于利用资源，对吧？
content.en_text: Amy: That's right, the Maori were very resourceful weren't they?

content.cn_text: Mike: 他们确实如此。
content.en_text: Mike: They certainly were.

groups.desc: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
groups.title: Questions 21–25

items.title: Amy and Mike agree that the greenstone tikis
items.analysis: 答案解析：第21题问 Amy 和 Mike 对绿石 tiki 达成什么一致。Mike 说能看出 skill involved，Amy 说需要 expertise and time，说明两人都认同制作需要高超技艺，所以答案是 A. take great skill to produce。

items.title: According to Amy, why are so few genuine old tikis found on archaeological dig sites?

options.title: A
options.content: Not many people know about them.

options.title: B
options.content: They tend to be stolen by treasure hunters.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第22题问 Amy 认为为什么考古遗址中很少发现真正古老的 tiki。原文说它们被拥有者保存，并 passed down through the generations，像 heirlooms，所以答案是 C. The majority become inherited items。

items.title: The Maori people considered tikis to be
items.analysis: 答案解析：第23题问毛利人认为 tiki 是什么。原文说 tikis were sacred，并与祖先、逝者沟通有关，所以答案是 B. religious objects。decoration 和 tribal symbols 都是被纠正或排除的信息。

items.title: Tikis are classified into one type or the other by
items.analysis: 答案解析：第24题问 tiki 是根据什么分为两类。原文说差异在于 feet, heads and hands 的摆放方式，即身体部位的位置，所以答案是 C. the position of the body。

items.title: How can modern reproductions be easily distinguished from genuine Maori carvings?

options.title: B
options.content: They are too regular in shape.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第25题问现代复制品如何容易与真正毛利雕刻区分。原文说机器和激光制作的复制品 no blemishes，而真品不完美，孔也不完全圆，说明复制品形状太规整，所以答案是 B. They are too regular in shape。

groups.desc: Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–E, next to Questions 26–30.
groups.title: Questions 26–30

items.title: creating a blank
items.analysis: 答案解析：第26题问 creating a blank 使用什么工具。原文说用 hard stone tool 划出凹槽并折断，对应选项 C. Stone scoring tool。

items.title: smoothing the surface
items.analysis: 答案解析：第27题问 smoothing the surface 使用什么工具。原文说用 sandstone block 反复摩擦绿石来准备雕刻，所以答案是 A. Sandstone block。

items.title: carving details
items.analysis: 答案解析：第28题问 carving details 使用什么工具。原文在讲切出手脚细节时说还使用 sharpened bone made into a point，对应选项 D. Bone point。

items.title: making holes
items.analysis: 答案解析：第29题问 making holes 使用什么工具。原文说 special tool 是 stick tied between pebbles，并用绳子来回拉动打孔，对应选项 E. Stick drill。

items.title: fixing coloured decorations
items.analysis: 答案解析：第30题问 fixing coloured decorations 使用什么材料。原文说用 tarata gum，一种 sticky plant resin，固定彩色装饰，对应选项 B. Plant glue。
